behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: This can fail at the prompt-construction/diff-filtering logic boundary and is observable entirely in-process by asserting what text is passed as review input.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a synthetic unified diff plus full file contents into the scoping function, then assert the produced review input includes only added/changed hunks and excludes unchanged regions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real PR diff-position semantics enforced by GitHub APIs/UI, which often differ from local assumptions.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions for specific changed lines, then read back PR review comments via API and verify each comment lands on the intended file/line in the diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is model judgment quality/non-deterministic output, which cannot be validated with deterministic non-LLM tests.
behaviors.test_description: Run the analysis model on a seeded PR containing a known bug (e.g., missing null check) multiple times with fixed settings; assert at least one run emits a finding that correctly flags the seeded defect.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic run-local referential-integrity check between summary payload and collected posted-comment IDs.
behaviors.test_description: Given an in-memory set of posted inline comment IDs and generated findings, build the summary payload and assert every summary entry references an ID present in that same run context.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: This is completeness of transformation/aggregation logic and can be caught by deterministic in-process assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Create a finding list with varied severities/files, run summary assembly, and assert cardinality and stable identity mapping from all inline findings to summary entries before the post call.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: This requires real end-to-end API interactions and ordering across external GitHub operations, but no live model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline against a real PR using a fixed finding fixture: fetch diff from GitHub, post inline comments, then post top-level summary; verify all API calls succeed and artifacts exist on the PR.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This validates the full sequential pipeline including an agentic analysis step plus downstream system actions, so the end-to-end workflow is the true failure surface.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger a live unattended run on a real PR: fetch diff, generate findings via model, post inline comments, publish summary, then validate completion criteria (non-empty coherent findings, successful postings, and linked summary/comment references).
